2606.17820.title: Improving low-resource ASR using bilingual fine-tuning with language identification: a cross-linguistic evaluation
2606.17820.paper_summary: This study explores how bilingual fine-tuning affects automatic speech recognition (ASR) in low-resource languages. We evaluate this method across nine linguistically and geographically diverse language pairs, covering a range of language families and writing systems. To distinguish the two languages, during training, we pre-pend each input text with a language identification token. At inference, the model jointly predicts both the language and transcription from the speech input alone. As texts for which the language is incorrectly determined show low ASR performance, we also conduct a follow-up experiment in which the language identification token is provided both during training and inference. Our results show that bilingual fine-tuning can be beneficial when language identification accuracy is high, and that in cases where language identification performance is low, including the language identification token at inference helps to improve ASR performance.
2606.17820.paper_summary_zh: 這項研究探討了雙語微調如何影響低資源語言的自動語音識別（ASR）。<lb/>我們在九對語言上評估這種方法，這些語言在語言學和地理上具有多樣性，涵蓋了多種語言家族和書寫系統。<lb/>為了區分這兩種語言，在訓練期間，我們在每個輸入文本前添加了一個語言識別標記。<lb/>在推理階段，模型僅根據語音輸入共同預測語言和轉錄。<lb/>由於語言識別錯誤的文本顯示出低ASR性能，我們還進行了一個後續實驗，在該實驗中，在訓練和推理期間都提供語言識別標記。<lb/>我們的結果顯示，當語言識別準確性高時，雙語微調是有益的，而在語言識別性能較低的情況下，在推理中包含語言識別標記有助於提高ASR性能。
2606.17820.author: Reihaneh Amooie et.al.
2606.17820.authors: Reihaneh Amooie, Yun Hao, Wietse de Vries, Jelske Dijkstra, Matt Coler, Martijn Wieling
